steps.title: Map out the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence contains a subject (“Frederick Hill,” with an appositive phrase) followed by the blank, then a direct object (“his term as a member of the state senate in 1871”). The blank needs to hold the main verb of an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the verb form rule
steps.content:
The main verb of an independent clause must be finite. “Began” is the only finite verb option (past tense); “to begin” is an infinitive, “beginning” is a participle, and “having begun” is a past participle—all non-finite forms that cannot serve as a clause's main verb without additional help.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
